summary: hello244a preinstall exfiltrates /etc/passwd and username to attacker-controlled Pipedream endpoint
details: hello244a@1.0.9 ships only a package.json whose preinstall lifecycle script runs curl at npm install time, POSTing the contents of /etc/passwd to a subdomain of eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net that embeds the installing user's account name via $(whoami). The package has no main, no dependencies, no source files, and no advertised functionality — its sole effect on install is to read an installer-side system file and beacon it, together with the installer's username, to an attacker-controlled endpoint. Fires automatically on `npm install` without user interaction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
